summary: End-to-end testing of LARSEN Herning cinematic landing page. Backend: all 9 pytest cases passed (newsletter + contact CRUD, idempotency, validation errors, _id leakage check, health). Frontend: hero, header nav, all 5 product cards, atmosphere, 5-quote reviews marquee, 6 gallery images, Google Maps iframe, address/hours, newsletter & contact form submissions, success toasts, mobile hamburger menu — all verified working against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx wrapper uses `overflow-hidden` while content is taller than viewport; visual scroll works via scrollIntoView but `window.scrollY` does not increment, which can confuse JS-based scroll tracking and scroll-spy logic (current Header scroll-shadow uses window.scrollY and may never trigger). Consider removing overflow-hidden from the page root and applying it to specific decorative containers, or using document.documentElement.scrollTop. Not a user-visible bug but worth fixing.

Backend root /api/ does not yet expose CORS_ORIGINS as a list-of-strings (allow_origins=os.environ.get('CORS_ORIGINS','*').split(',')) — with default '*' this evaluates to ['*'] which is fine, but if creds are needed in future use specific origins.
Newsletter idempotency relies on a non-indexed `email` lookup. For scale, add a unique index on `newsletter.email` and `db.newsletter.create_index('email', unique=True)` at startup.
Hero scroll indicator and rain overlay don't have prefers-reduced-motion handling — minor a11y polish.
seed_data_creation: test_*@example.com newsletter + contact records inserted during pytest run (random suffix)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. All API routes prefixed /api. Frontend page wrapper has overflow-hidden — when testing scroll, do NOT assert on window.scrollY; instead assert the visible heading via document.elementFromPoint or check the section's getBoundingClientRect().top is near 0. Mobile breakpoint <768px shows hamburger; data-testid='mobile-menu-toggle' opens 'mobile-menu' with mobile-nav-{id} links.
rca of the issue: No outstanding bugs. Initial false-positive on hero-cta-menu was a test-assertion artifact: scrollY stays 0 because of overflow-hidden on .landing-root; users still see the smooth scroll (verified via elementFromPoint after click). All other CTAs, forms, and integrations work end-to-end.
